experiments.purpose: Probe the capability-lift pattern's recipe, super-protocol unification options (A refinement, B parameterized Carrier<Underlying>, C both), API broadening at four levels, and limits (generic Underlying, ~Copyable Underlying, existentials). Six variants V0–V5.
experiments.statusDetail: V0 Cardinal baseline, V1 Ordinal baseline with refinement, V2 super-protocol via refinement, V3 super-protocol via SE-0346 parameterization, V4 API broadening at four levels, V5 limits (three sub-cases catalogued). Moved from swift-institute/Experiments/ on 2026-04-24 when swift-carrier-primitives was created.

experiments.purpose: Empirically test whether Span<Element>: Carrier is viable in the Q3 (Copyable × ~Escapable) quadrant, contra Research/sli-span-family.md DECISION (2026-04-24). Four variants: V1 default extension, V2 explicit witnesses same-module, V3 cross-module use, V4 release-mode validation per [EXP-017].
experiments.statusDetail: V1 REFUTED (default `Carrier where Underlying == Self` extension is gated to Self: Escapable; ~Escapable Self witness candidates are excluded entirely). V2/V3/V4 CONFIRMED with explicit witnesses (`@_lifetime(borrow self) _read { yield self }` + `@_lifetime(copy underlying) init(_:)`). Cross-module generic algorithms returning `C.Underlying` as a function result require `where C.Underlying: Copyable`. The production default extension does NOT cover Span; per-Span explicit bodies are required (~5 lines each). Refines sli-span-family.md concern #1 with a sharper failure mode and confirms concern #3 (explicit bodies, not one-liners).

narrativeSections.heading: Provenance
narrativeSections.body: This experiment was authored in swift-institute/Experiments/ on 2026-04-22 and moved to this package on 2026-04-24. See Research/capability-lift-pattern.md §Methodology for variant verdicts.
